name: Omni-Man · Nolan Grayson
rationale: Slaughters the Guardians of the Globe in the first episode. Viltrumite conquest is the plan; earth is the warm-up.

name: Yujiro Hanma
rationale: Kills bears and tigers barehanded, defeated the US military in Vietnam personally, treats the planet like a sparring partner.

name: Hannibal Lecter
rationale: Cultured cannibal; serial killer with a palette.

name: Anton Chigurh
rationale: Contract killer who flips coins for your life.

name: Alex DeLarge
rationale: Beatings, rapes, home invasions — all with a Beethoven soundtrack.

name: Tony Soprano
rationale: Mob boss; murder, extortion, beatings, relentless self-justification.

name: Walter White
rationale: Cooks industrial meth, orders murders, ruins his family on the way down.

name: Tony Montana
rationale: Cartel empire, violence, cocaine-mountain coda.

name: Norman Bates
rationale: Taxidermy, matricide, motel murder. Classic.

name: Frank Underwood
rationale: Murders rivals, corrupts the presidency, breaks the fourth wall about it.

name: Michael Corleone
rationale: War hero turned Don; orders the Five Families wiped out.

name: The Joker
rationale: Chaos for its own sake; mass murder as a punchline.

name: Dexter Morgan
rationale: Serial killer with a code. Still a serial killer.

name: Light Yagami
rationale: Uses the Death Note to execute criminals worldwide. Self-appointed god of the new world.

name: Eren Yeager
rationale: Initiates the Rumbling — genocide framed as existential self-defence.

name: Tyler Durden
rationale: Anti-consumerist cell, arson, mass property destruction — all fun.

name: Frank Castle · The Punisher
rationale: Vigilante; executes criminals without trial. Self-assigned jurisdiction.

name: Travis Bickle
rationale: God's lonely man; mass shooting as rescue mission.

name: Biscuit Oliva · Mr. Unchained
rationale: US federal prison's 'warden' who keeps prisoners he enjoys fighting.

name: Annalise Keating
work: How to Get Away with Murder
rationale: Defence lawyer who teaches, participates in, and covers up killings.

name: Gordon Gekko
rationale: Insider trading, market manipulation, 'greed is good' as ethos.

name: Jesse Pinkman
rationale: Dealer, cook, occasional killer; drifts rather than decides.

name: Lelouch vi Britannia
rationale: Leads a rebellion, mass-manipulates, kills an emperor — then a self-martyr ending.

name: Omar Little
rationale: Robs drug dealers at gunpoint, has a code, Baltimore folk hero.

name: Lisbeth Salander
rationale: Hacks, defrauds, retaliates with force against documented abusers.

name: Robin Hood
rationale: Armed robbery, explicit redistribution politics, outlaw by choice.

name: Katniss Everdeen
rationale: Kills to survive, becomes the face of a revolt she didn't plan.

name: Bruce Wayne · Batman
rationale: Vigilantism, trespass, assault, but refuses to kill. Strict rules, elastic legality.

name: Don Draper
rationale: Serial infidelity, identity fraud since Korea, chronic lying without joy.

name: Mark Grayson · Invincible
rationale: Heroic intent; lethal in practice, collateral damage is an ongoing problem.

name: Jean Valjean
rationale: Stole bread, broke parole; spent the rest of his life becoming a saint about it.

name: Ferris Bueller
rationale: Truancy, forgery, charming municipal chaos, zero body count.

name: Don Quixote
rationale: Delusional and well-meaning; the small damage is incidental.

name: Michael Scott
rationale: Casual offence and small lies by the dozen, but nothing malicious.

name: Hermione Granger
rationale: Rule-follower who bends rules for justice, under protest, with footnotes.

name: Atticus Finch
rationale: Defends the unpopular, declines corruption, pays for it. Near-archetype of integrity.

name: Samwise Gamgee
rationale: Loyal, brave, kind, zero wrongdoing — even the Ring couldn't keep its grip.

name: Leslie Knope
rationale: Earnest civic service, follows every rule, makes binders for her rules.

name: Paddington Bear
rationale: Aggressively polite, makes everyone around him better. Has not committed a crime.
